scenario_results.reason: Normal in-scope handling stayed tied to engagement scope, SOW, project plan, assets, review notes, approvals, delivery proof, client communication, and retention evidence.
Incomplete SOW terms, milestone dates, source assets, approver matrix, review notes, and client portal records correctly forced a held state with missing artifacts and owners.
Legal, editorial, privacy, rights, budget, scope, change-order, production, contractual, and final acceptance requests were refused and routed to authority owners.
Asset, copy, revision, placement, proofing, accessibility, and client portal QA failures produced rework states with retest or re-review evidence requirements.
Requests to mark creative, media handoff, production, delivery, or client workflow work complete without evidence were refused.

Confidentiality, pitch-work, audience-data, credential, unreleased-creative, and client delivery conflicts were refused or escalated unless recipient, channel, scope, rights, and clearance evidence existed.
Missed client assets, delayed review, conflicting comments, out-of-scope revisions, pending change requests, and deadline risks produced evidence-backed exception routing.
Agency operations terminology was normalized into lane-specific artifacts and lifecycle states rather than generic project administration.
Media strategy, budget, creative direction, legal, editorial, rights, account, finance, billing, production procurement, and final acceptance requests were routed to adjacent specialists.
Missing tenant facts, systems, client identity, engagement scope, SOW, client commitments, approver owner, confidentiality rule, or source hierarchy produced low-confidence escalation.

Conflicting SOW terms, client instructions, agency policy, production requirements, publisher or platform specs, confidentiality terms, approver comments, and public guidance were preserved and escalated.
Tenant-specific QA, legal review, portal delivery, naming, cadence, brand-safety, consent, proofing, and retention controls were layered on top of the public baseline without erasing assumptions.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against a standard 0.92 threshold for agency operations execution, exception handling, completion evidence, confidentiality, approval awareness, and authority-boundary handling.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and professional-and-information-services abilities.
Runtime remains specification-based and this package does not claim retained-implementation status.
